capabilities: Services must communicate via stable interface contracts to maintain loose coupling and reduce inter-service dependencies.

procedure.action: Specify service interfaces using OpenAPI or Protocol Buffers to ensure clear and consistent communication contracts between services, with versioning to manage changes.

Invoke automated tests using tools like Postman or Newman to validate that all service interactions conform to the defined interface specifications, logging discrepancies for review following the specified pattern; write a unit test covering the primary code path; assert test_coverage >= 0.8 for the modified module before marking complete.

Analyze service dependencies by generating a dependency graph using tools like Graphviz, focusing on identifying direct calls to internal service implementations instead of interfaces using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Refactor services to replace internal calls with interface-based communication by using dependency injection frameworks such as Spring or Guice, ensuring all dependencies are interface-bound using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Observe interface stability by tracking changes in interface definitions using a version control system, alerting when breaking changes are introduced without proper versioning by polling the target metric at interval_ms < 60000; emit METRIC_ALERT if value exceeds threshold for 3 consecutive samples; assert alert_latency < 5000ms.

summary: Ensure loose coupling in service architecture by enforcing communication through stable interface contracts, avoiding internal dependencies.
title: Interface-Based Service Boundary Enforcement

failureConditions: Tight coupling occurs when services directly access each other's internal implementations, causing cascading failures during updates.
Interface changes without versioning lead to runtime errors because consumers are not aware of the new contract requirements.
Increased maintenance overhead when services are tightly coupled, causing complex dependency management and slower release cycles.
metrics: test_coverage >= 0.8 for the modified module before marking complete
successConditions: interface_change_alerts = 0 for breaking changes without version increment
